title: I Gained It So,
content:
I gained it so,
    By climbing slow,
    By catching at the twigs that grow
    Between the bliss and me.
    It hung so high,
    As well the sky
    Attempt by strategy.


    I said I gained it, --
    This was all.
    Look, how I clutch it,
    Lest it fall,
    And I a pauper go;
    Unfitted by an instant's grace
    For the contented beggar's face
    I wore an hour ago.
meaning:
Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "I Gained It So,"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
